In Countries like China and Japan, fish have been stored in rice soaked vinegar since medieval times. This was originally done to keep the fish from spoiling, but eventually Japanese people began eating the rice with the fish. By the 1800s, around when the first trains were invented, sushi as we know it today was invented by Hanaya Yohei.

column10:
A horse is a big animal with four strong legs, hooves, and long hair on its head, neck, and tail. Horses are mammals, like humans, who drink milk from their moms when they’re babies. A baby horse is called a foal and can stand and walk within the first hours it is born. Horses are strong runners with good balance who can bend down to sip water, eat grass(es), and even sleep while standing up.

column10: Sunglasses are special lenses people wear to protect their eyes and help them see when there is bright light. Sunglass lenses are made of tinted glass that filters or collects some of the light before it reaches your eyes--allowing some but not all light to pass through.
